meta.question: Are real-estate developers paying Austin politicians?
meta.pillLabel: One firm, two decades of checks

event.question: Are real-estate developers paying Austin politicians?

event.description: Identify and summarize state-level financial relationships between Kirk Watson and Endeavor Real Estate Group using TEC campaign-finance records.

source.rowSummary: Texas Ethics Commission filer index, filerIdent 00023391: Watson, Kirk P. (The Honorable) (COH).
TEC campaign-finance report 100692094, contribution to Watson, Kirk P. (The Honorable) from Rudy, Kirk: $2,500, 20180116.
TEC campaign-finance report 438928, contribution to Watson, Kirk P. from Rudy, Kirk: $2,500, 20100115.
TEC campaign-finance report 299241, contribution to Watson, Kirk P. from Roche, David: $2,500, 20060117.

event.description: Write up the pattern of Endeavor-related contributions to Kirk Watson’s state filer and summarize the financial relationship.

event.text: Two decades of $1,000 to $2,500 checks from one Austin real-estate firm into the same state filer.

citations.rowSummary: TEC campaign-finance report 205674, contribution to Watson, Kirk from Rudy, Kirk: $1,000, 20021007.

event.text: Endeavor Real Estate Group shows up in Texas Ethics Commission records as a steady source of contributions to Kirk Watson’s state filer, with Endeavor-linked contributors repeatedly writing checks in the $1,000–$2,500 range from at least 2002 through 2020, indicating a long-running financial relationship rather than a one-off contribution.

citations.rowSummary: TEC campaign-finance report 205674, contribution to Watson, Kirk from Rudy, Kirk: $1,000, 20021007.
TEC campaign-finance report 100768027, contribution to Watson, Kirk P. (The Honorable) from Miller, Bryce: $1,000, 20200115.

event.text: The Endeavor-tied giving to Watson’s committee spans his time in the Legislature and beyond, clustering around January semiannual reports and fall campaign periods, with multiple $2,500 entries in 2006, 2010, 2012, and 2018 and repeat $1,000–$2,000 checks in other years, suggesting Endeavor’s principals treated Watson as a recurring state-level ally worth supporting over multiple cycles.

citations.rowSummary: TEC campaign-finance report 299241, contribution to Watson, Kirk P. from Roche, David: $2,500, 20060117.
TEC campaign-finance report 100692094, contribution to Watson, Kirk P. (The Honorable) from Rudy, Kirk: $2,500, 20180116.

event.question: Which other Texas politicians get money from Endeavor Real Estate?
event.kicker: FOLLOW THE FIRM, NOT THE FILER
event.rationale: Endeavor's principals have written checks to Watson for two decades. The next step trims off Watson and asks who else collects from the same firm — same employer string, different filerNames.
